design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate vertically scrolling airspace, destroy enemy formations, collect power-ups, reach boss encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing screen real estate, dodging projectile patterns while maintaining offensive pressure
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Piloting a military aircraft through hostile territory with escalating aerial combat challenges
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Classic arcade shmup philosophy emphasizing pattern recognition and spatial awareness over resource management; focuses on pure shooting mechanics within a military aesthetic

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives with continue system typical of arcade-style shooters
difficulty.continues_system: Standard continue mechanics allowing progression after game over

progression.upgrade_system: Power-up drops from destroyed enemies provide temporary weapon and defensive enhancements
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage progression through boss defeat unlocks subsequent levels
replayability_hooks: Score chasing and high score competitions
Learning complex enemy patterns for faster runs

world_building_techniques: Military setting implied through aircraft design and enemy aesthetics

boss_design.philosophy: Stage-culminating aerial encounters with distinct mechanical patterns; bosses serve as difficulty gates between progression stages

environmental_storytelling: Progressive stage environments indicate advance through hostile territory

extraction_notes: Limited source material available. The Cutting Room Floor reference provided only navigation/informational content without substantive game design documentation. Data extracted from game paradigm, platform, and year context using standard shmup genre conventions. T2 tier assessment appropriate—qualitative descriptors only, no specific mechanics documentation available in provided sources.
